title: I attended my first web analytics event as a freelancer and it hit differently.
description: I joined Measurecamp Amsterdam, which was also my first time experiencing the MeasureCamp format. Coming from mostly SEO conferences, this was a shift in more ways than one.

title: I’ll be at Measurecamp Amsterdam this Saturday (tomorrow).
description: Looking forward to learning more about AI traffic, measurement setups and how people are adapting their analytics in this space.

title: I’ve been working on an AI traffic dashboard and turned it into a free Looker Studio template that anyone can use (link in comments).
description: How it works: 1. Open the template, 2. Click 'Edit & Share' at the top right, 3. Select in the data control at the top left of the page the GA4 property of your choice.

title: Talking to heavy AI users made me look up what the broader public is actually doing with AI
description: Some of my coworkers were heavy AI users. One had built his own agent and even used it to reduce his screen time. Another one was deeply into coding.

title: Interesting analysis from Kevin Indig on how AI selects its sources.
description: One point stood out to me: Ranking well helps, but doesn’t guarantee citation. That’s very much in line with what I’ve been seeing in AI traffic reporting.

title: I recently came across a category of tools that promise better attribution. Even without user consent or cookies.
description: The idea is: user data, transform it (for ex. hashing, transforming identifiers, etc.) and use it as an “anonymous” identifier.

title: How to know if our SEO (and LLM) web efforts actually drive app conversions? (A rough guideline to get an idea)
description: In app-first businesses, this is hard to answer because attribution often breaks when the website hands the user over to the App Store / Google Play.

title: My biggest “fail” in Web Analytics is actually a repeating one and that’s exactly why I think it’s valuable.
description: Every few months, I catch myself doing the same thing: Going deep into analysis… and initially overlooking what’s happening in plain sight.

title: I often get asked what the “latest insights” are when it comes to SEO and AI.
description: There isn’t one simple answer. But this guide by Jo Furnival is a good starting point for one aspect of it. The idea that stands out: Content doesn’t just need to rank. It needs to be usable by models.
